pains.summary: Missing on forecast, no reliable mechanism to predict revenue
pains.quote: There's been like a lot of frustration, I think both at like the KKR level and the ELT level on like, you know, missing on forecast, right. And like just not having like a really reliable mechanism to like say, Hey, here's what we're going to do.
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Sales reps piping opportunities as renewals when they're actually new business
pains.quote: I have sales reps that for some reason, when they pipe opportunities, they're all coming up as renewals. And how do we reclassify it when it's actually new biz?
pains.speaker: E.T. Mac

pains.summary: Lack of process attaching right contacts to opportunities causing lost attribution
pains.quote: we lack process on the sales side, Attaching the right contacts to opportunities. That's a big source of lost attribution for us
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: Messy data with duplicate leads, duplicate accounts, general data hygiene issues
pains.quote: we have a lot of data complexity and a lot of messy data, like specifically duplicate leads, duplicate accounts, just a lot of like just general data hygiene issues that are not gonna be cleaned up in the near term.
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: Salesforce integration blocked by internal IT/security delays
pains.quote: the project is delayed until we get Snowflake and Salesforce integrated.
pains.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

pains.summary: Previous CMO let go, new CMO Brian Wallace just started, creating planning flux
pains.quote: We have a new CMO that started a month and a half ago. We're right in the midst, I would say, of like second half of the year planning, which I know we're late to the game there, but related to, you know, new marketing leadership in place.
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: No tool to measure marketing effectiveness, leading to skepticism about marketing investment
pains.quote: up until recently, there's been a high degree of skepticism around marketing investment, because we haven't had a tool like this
pains.speaker: Brian Wallace

pains.summary: Sales team doesn't attach contacts to opportunities properly, making attribution difficult
pains.quote: 20 or 30 percent of our opportunities don't even have a contact associated. We should probably figure out what we need to do in terms of reporting to account for that because we're lucky if we get one, maybe two contacts on a given opportunity
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: Legacy brand traffic being misattributed, hiding true marketing value
pains.quote: this is going to show that like what four million dollars and some change almost 4.5 million dollars of pipeline in this period came from people looking for ihs by name
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Third-party cookie issues causing UTM stripping and attribution gaps for paid media
pains.quote: our utms have been getting stripped out by the cookie solution
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: No consistent naming conventions for campaigns making channel classification impossible
pains.quote: there's no consistency, right? Because automated emails marketing tagged emails that are cx in nature and then there's like things that kind of follow that same structure that like our marketing in nature and so like realistically i think to do what you're trying to do which is the right thing to do we need kind of like a broader conversation with the organization and like some parameters for naming conventions in the very least right
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Leads not being converted to contacts, losing data and creating duplicates
pains.quote: the leads aren't being converted to contact which aren't being attached to opportunities but like in some cases right like if a duplicate contact is created along that way we then lose utm in that instance as well
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Zero dollar opportunities making pipeline/revenue reporting unreliable
pains.quote: you'll see like this big deficit in the bookings that doesn't line up to like the volume numbers on like the opportunity and the leads, like the revenue is not where you would expect it to be. Right. But then you double click into these things and you'll just see it's just because there's a ton of $0 opportunities.
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: No sales accepted lead stage exists, creating confusion in funnel definitions
pains.quote: i see stage called sales accepted lead but i don't think this actually exists like in salesforce or any of our other systems
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: Email marketing inflated by CX and retail emails, not true marketing
pains.quote: email marketing showing $31 million, which seems like a lot compared to like the level of focus, I guess it is for marketing as a channel. So that's where I want to really like dive into some of these bigger categories. Cause I'm, my assumption is that there's probably a bunch of like CX emails.
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: HubSpot shared with CX and retail teams, causing data commingling
pains.quote: that email marketing, the top row here gets, there's a lot of revenue and pipeline tied up there, but it's ultimately like, you know, going back to the whole HubSpot conversation being shared with CX and retail, like all of that is kind of lumped together as email marketing
pains.speaker: Dana Silverman

pains.summary: Opportunity naming conventions unreliable for new logo vs expansion classification
pains.quote: we are supposed to be able to rely on if new logo or new business is in an opportunity name, then we know that's a new logo. That is not the case, right? There is not any kind of uniformity on the opportunity naming convention
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Bing integration blocked by IT resource constraints
pains.quote: we are spending money on Bing and we are relying on Nirav in IT to integrate Bing. We have had a lot of difficulty getting him to do that.
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Paid media agency limited to lead-level data, inaccurate for optimization
pains.quote: the paid media agency right now is limited because of the UTM situation to only looking at like leads and even the leads aren't really. accurate.
pains.speaker: Michael Seymour

pains.summary: Channel classifications in RevSure showing wrong business line attributions
pains.quote: i would see a lot of like engineering intelligence spend and then like i was on with our product manager showing her and she was like that's not that's way over budget it's showing the ei campaigns here too so those are actually are not hitting the cost center
pains.speaker: Nick Davis

tools_switched_from.reason: Cookie banner causing massive attribution blind spots by stripping UTMs
tools_switched_from.quote: there's a new landscape, want to see additional efficiencies. And then one you kind of threw out there at the last second to us was, I have sales reps that for some reason, when they pipe opportunities, they're all coming up as renewals.
tools_switched_from.speaker: E.T. Mac

tools_switched_from.reason: CFO using Power BI for forecasting but it's difficult and not reliable; seeking better source of truth
tools_switched_from.quote: Pending thing right now that we're trying to refinance into this is like, I don't know what systems. They use, I know they're using power BI, right. For like the end all sorts of truth, which is difficult.
tools_switched_from.speaker: Michael Seymour

tools_switched_from.reason: Eliminated because they don't do cookieless tracking
tools_switched_from.quote: we talked to caliber mind but they said they don't do cookie list ranking so they're out.
tools_switched_from.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

political_tussles.tension_label: Attribution credit dispute between marketing and sales
political_tussles.summary: CMO Brian Wallace does not want marketing to take 100% credit for any opportunity; wants sales touchpoints included to show sales impact
political_tussles.quote: our CMO doesn't want marketing, whatever funnel we choose. um or a model we choose he doesn't want marketing to take 100 credit of any like opportunity so um we need to make sure that however we're building our model that we're adding in those sales touch points as well because he wants to show uh sales um impact on an opportunity as well
political_tussles.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

political_tussles.tension_label: Executive pressure on marketing spend scrutiny after CMO change
political_tussles.summary: New CMO under pressure from CEO/CFO to justify marketing dollars, especially after previous leader was let go
political_tussles.quote: the marketing team is being scrutinized on our dollars that we're spending, especially since they let go of our previous leader.
political_tussles.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

political_tussles.tension_label: Contact consolidation conflict - CX trying to bring contacts into HubSpot unrelated to marketing's attribution needs
political_tussles.summary: CX team trying to bring contacts into HubSpot for administrative/renewal purposes, creating confusion about marketable contacts vs. non-marketable contacts and potential cost increases
political_tussles.quote: CX is trying to bring contacts into HubSpot, not really related to what we're doing, but like they're trying to consolidate platforms in the same vein.
political_tussles.speaker: Michael Seymour

political_tussles.tension_label: attribution ownership and credit for deals
political_tussles.summary: Michael argues sales and marketing side by side shows 80% of new logo deals originated from marketing, pushing back on MSR-only view
political_tussles.quote: when you look at sales and marketing side by side it is like Abundantly clear that 80% of the new logo deals if not more originated from marketing
political_tussles.speaker: Michael Seymour

political_tussles.tension_label: who owns HubSpot and email sends
political_tussles.summary: Dana wants to exclude CX and sales emails to get pure marketing view, but system commingling makes this difficult
political_tussles.quote: yeah if we're going to do that we might want to pull out like sales and admin cx like if we're if the goal here is just trying to get like a marketing view by chance
political_tussles.speaker: Dana Silverman

political_tussles.tension_label: HubSpot ownership and data swim lanes
political_tussles.summary: Nick suggests drawing swim lanes around marketing-controlled data vs non-marketing data to manage uncertainty
political_tussles.quote: I don't know if we need to draw at some point some swim lanes and say we're confident in the marketing data, but we don't have a point of contact and there's a level of uncertainty around non-marketing data.
political_tussles.speaker: Nick Davis

political_tussles.summary: Nick wants to set different MQL thresholds for inbound vs outbound leads based on conversion rate differences
political_tussles.quote: an inbound lead that's doing their own research coming filling out a demo form might actually convert to the bottom of the funnel at a higher clip than these outbound raw leads and so we might actually need less mqls if they're inbound than outbound
political_tussles.speaker: Nick Davis

contrarians.claim: Willing to share that RevSure will become infrastructural and impossible to remove - not in client's best interest as negotiating tactic
contrarians.why_unusual: Clients typically hide their lock-in vulnerability; explicitly stating the vendor will become sticky is unusual transparency that could weaken negotiating position
contrarians.quote: I hate to even share this because it's not in our best interest as the client, it's not going to be able to go away. There's going to be persistence. And what that means on a midterm renewal or renewal or whatever, I think it's perfect.
contrarians.speaker: Michael Seymour

contrarians.claim: Linear attribution with equal credit is best practice for B2B with hundreds/thousands of touchpoints
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors push position-based (W/U-shaped) or data-driven models; advocating linear as default for complex B2B journeys is contrarian
contrarians.quote: linear with equal credit. What we typically see, Brian, is that customers, when you look at a journey from, you know, new logo journey in particular, you know, from the first touch to a closed one deal, it could be hundreds, maybe even thousands of touch points. Giving all the credit to one or two or three or five touch points just seems like you're losing all of the influence and all of the work
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: MQLs are 'stupid' and CMO hates them
contrarians.why_unusual: Despite MQL being standard B2B metric, CMO openly dismisses them; team considers dropping MQL-focused reporting entirely
contrarians.quote: our CMO talks a lot like he thinks that he hates mqls he thinks they're like stupid I don't know so at this point I don't know if he's actually going to truly care about that
contrarians.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

contrarians.claim: RevSure may not be the best place for funnel conversion rate reporting - Salesforce might be better
contrarians.why_unusual: Typically MTA vendors position themselves as the single source of truth for all funnel metrics; client pushes back that stage progression belongs in CRM
contrarians.quote: I just wonder if, like, Rebshare is the best place to do that. Like, if it should be done in Salesforce or something. Because I think we've got it, like, in other places where there's, like, maybe less chance where the data gets, like, construed.
contrarians.speaker: Dana Silverman

contrarians.claim: Want to exclude email marketing entirely from channel reporting because CX contamination makes it unusable
contrarians.why_unusual: Email is typically a core channel; client would rather have no email data than wrong email data
contrarians.quote: i can't use email because over inflate our number so i'd rather just take it out
contrarians.speaker: Nick Davis

contrarians.claim: Stage zero opportunities with no dollar amounts may become more common, not less
contrarians.why_unusual: Contradicts typical push to always have opportunity values; client considering zero-dollar stage as valid process step
contrarians.quote: there's conversations around zero dollar opportunities right now as maybe like a thing we actually might be using more as opposed to less as sort of like a stage zero
contrarians.speaker: Dana Silverman

revsure_problems.problem: Salesforce integration blocked/delayed by client's internal IT, but causing project delay
revsure_problems.quote: the project is delayed until we get Snowflake and Salesforce integrated.
revsure_problems.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

revsure_problems.problem: Client unclear on how attribution models work in practice - whether last touch is static or dynamic as opportunity progresses
revsure_problems.quote: if it's in if it's open in open pipeline is the last touch always moving? Like, is it always going to change until it closes or is it static?
revsure_problems.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

revsure_problems.problem: Cannot currently customize W model to restrict primary touches to specific touch types (e.g., only sales for last touch) - requires backend customization
revsure_problems.quote: in terms of customization of the model it's something that we can do but it's not something that you can play around in the ui
revsure_problems.speaker: Shantanu

revsure_problems.problem: Cannot currently do view-through attribution for LinkedIn ad impressions at account level without engagement
revsure_problems.quote: Currently, no, because it's one by one. But when we do the enhancement to allow you to customize the model, I don't know that that specifically is in the roadmap
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Custom W-shaped model not yet live, causing zeroed-out reports and blocking executive presentation
revsure_problems.quote: your custom model is still being tested out. Right now it's being reflected in the UI. but its full implementation has not occurred yet so just wanted to give you that um that's why we're showing zero
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: LinkedIn ads showing inflated $25M due to form bucketing issues; needs investigation
revsure_problems.quote: linkedin ads one is uh that's we got to roll that other category out it's 25 million from linkedin ads is uh definitely not going to be here
revsure_problems.speaker: Michael Seymour

revsure_problems.problem: OneTrust cookie scanning not picking up all cookies, requiring manual addition
revsure_problems.quote: only one of the cookie got accepted, which is our test cookie. But there are multiple other cookies which should be scanned and used by the website
revsure_problems.speaker: Sahil Gupta - RevSure

revsure_problems.problem: Client confused by difference between 'visitors' and 'visits from known contacts' metrics
revsure_problems.quote: what's the difference uh what's the difference between number of visitors and visits from known contacts
revsure_problems.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

revsure_problems.problem: Channel classification showing marketing emails from Salesforce as email marketing channel
revsure_problems.quote: i'm even seeing emails going out from salesforce and i'm like well marketing doesn't send emails from salesforce so we should exclude kind of like two marketing channels or two channels sorry you know one sales one marketing
revsure_problems.speaker: Nick Davis

revsure_problems.problem: Campaign attribution incorrectly assigning engineering intelligence campaigns to supply chain cost center
revsure_problems.quote: i would see a lot of like engineering intelligence spend and then like i was on with our product manager showing her and she was like that's not that's way over budget it's showing the ei campaigns here too so those are actually are not hitting the cost center
revsure_problems.speaker: Nick Davis

revsure_problems.problem: Channel classification rules not visible in platform, locked on backend
revsure_problems.quote: where are these like channel classifications and how can we review them quickly to get them to a place like where we all feel comfortable that's what i'm checking
revsure_problems.speaker: Nick Davis

revsure_problems.problem: Custom dimension for business unit segmentation broken, cannot save edits
revsure_problems.quote: request failed all right i may have to rebuild this for you
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Client unsure if default view shows all contacts or just primary contact on account
revsure_problems.quote: that's probably like that i'm only getting one contact if i don't set that
revsure_problems.speaker: Michael Seymour

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure's cookieless tracking was decisive factor in selection over competitors
best_about_revsure.quote: Those were, you guys are the only contenders because you guys could do the cookieless tracking and obviously we have that major cookie problem
best_about_revsure.speaker: Kristina Eisenhart

best_about_revsure.summary: Brian Wallace extremely excited about solution capabilities, getting 'butterflies'
best_about_revsure.quote: I could not be more excited, just even what you're doing right now. I'm literally like I'm getting butterflies in my stomach, but this is so good. We need this so bad
best_about_revsure.speaker: Brian Wallace

best_about_revsure.summary: Timing of RevSure arrival perfectly aligned with new private-equity growth mandate
best_about_revsure.quote: you guys coming in at this time could be more perfect because you guys are giving us insights into new logo opportunities we did not have before
best_about_revsure.speaker: Brian Wallace

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure captures touchpoints that other platforms miss due to OneTrust
best_about_revsure.quote: rev tries to solve for the lack of utms by using fingerprinting i'm sure you're familiar with this concept it's like the same thing as like a hockey stack right it's like a um not using a cookie but using characteristics of someone to identify them that's compliant through a gdpr lens
best_about_revsure.speaker: Michael Seymour

best_about_revsure.summary: Platform much more sophisticated and cleaner than previous tools
best_about_revsure.quote: the platform is leaps and bounds better than what i've get the best, excuse me, get the best out of it
best_about_revsure.speaker: Isaac Archer

best_about_revsure.summary: Ability to look at all contacts on account changes attribution story dramatically
best_about_revsure.quote: with rep share you can look at all contacts on the account when you open up that world the influence and the impact of linkedin grows exponentially right
best_about_revsure.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

best_about_revsure.summary: Journey timeline view helps resolve deal contention between teams
best_about_revsure.quote: if for whatever reason there's any kind of contention over a deal right so you can actually see like at the channel level where the influence happened that like led to this being called
best_about_revsure.speaker: Michael Seymour

immediate.win: RevSure selected as front runner over HockeyStack for ELT team
immediate.quote: it seems like the ELT team is like very interested in RevShare. It seems like you're a front runner over HockeyStack.
immediate.speaker: Michael Seymour

immediate.win: Pricing negotiated down from ~$125K to target low $80s for 700K contacts with forecasting
immediate.quote: BlizzPrice right now, for you guys, is definitely looking close to $125k. I'm going to really try to work to get this closer to $80k
immediate.speaker: E.T. Mac

immediate.win: Pipeline and booking attribution now visible by channel with ability to compare models
immediate.quote: we're looking at all the different campaign channels, have led to pipeline and booking with visibility into the funnel and of what was generated, where are they currently open in the pipeline, right? In which stage are they in right now? Looking at a linear model
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Historical data import capability confirmed for 2+ years of data for planning
immediate.quote: assuming we get all of our data into Rebshure in the next 30 days or so, will we have the ability to use, say, the last couple of years worth of our own data to do things like go forward projections by channel
immediate.speaker: Dana Silverman

immediate.win: Cookieless tracking script deployed to address OneTrust UTM stripping
immediate.quote: we deployed the official cookie list tracking side script for Acris, so everything after that
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Custom W-shape attribution model built with Acris team
immediate.quote: with the actress team we created a custom w-shaped model so how the credit is distributed is depending on different milestones
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Marketing influence pipeline dashboard showing filtered marketing-only channels
immediate.quote: i looked at your qc msv and i just like selected those channels so now it's truly um what is showing you is marketing influence
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Opportunity prioritization view created for closed-won deal inspection
immediate.quote: you can use this module which is a widget prioritization opportunity prioritization
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: New logo filtering logic implemented to exclude expansion/add-on op types
immediate.quote: I made the filter so that it's you have full visibility and it excluded like the add-on the standard and all those op types
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

long_term.win: Channel-level ROI understanding for CFO investment decisions
long_term.quote: they just want channel level understanding of if I got $50,000 to invest somewhere and I need the max return, where do I put it? Right at the CFO level.
long_term.speaker: Michael Seymour

long_term.win: Replace unreliable Power BI forecasting with RevSure's predictive capabilities
long_term.quote: he obviously will eventually use this as probably a more reliable source of truth than the Power BI dashboard he's using once he looks at both the estimates and realizes you guys are so much closer to right.
long_term.speaker: Michael Seymour

long_term.win: Massive expansion with target accounts and contact growth planned
long_term.quote: knowing that we'll be like a massive expansion and like target account effectively, like with all these contacts we're adding in like the next month
long_term.speaker: Michael Seymour

long_term.win: Board-level credibility for marketing investment with science-based measurement
long_term.quote: you're going to have CEO, CFO, and board level attention on this really, really quickly in a good way
long_term.speaker: Brian Wallace

long_term.win: 2026 planning confidence with channel-level ROI projections
long_term.quote: we're just gonna be starting to kick off 2026 planning. And this is going to be another level of confidence building to say, Every dollar you give us, we'll be able to tell you how effective it was going forward. No more guesswork
long_term.speaker: Brian Wallace

long_term.win: Unified sales + marketing narrative replacing 'sales vs marketing' framing
long_term.quote: we wanna show that it's not sales versus marketing, it's here's how the symbiotic relationship works
long_term.speaker: Brian Wallace

long_term.win: Push RevSure data to Power BI for broader organizational reporting
long_term.quote: probably like the longer term end state which i believe is ultimately like pushing this into power bi and like including with the rev ops reporting more
long_term.speaker: Dana Silverman

long_term.win: Separate inbound and outbound funnel tracking with different conversion expectations
long_term.quote: I'm almost thinking of two funnels in parallel and inbound marketing funnel with stages and then an outbound motion marketing funnel of with stages
long_term.speaker: Nick Davis

long_term.win: Agentic AI for lead scoring and rep prioritization alerts
long_term.quote: you can have agents in rapture right like look at all the signals and then like fire prioritization like if an account has reached this scoring right like if based on all the factors has reached this score this lead or has reached this score then like fire like an email or send the alert to the rep to reach out to them
long_term.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

before_state.pain_summary: Antiquated operations with no reliable forecasting, massive attribution blind spots from OneTrust cookie banner stripping 60-70% of UTMs, messy Salesforce data with duplicates and misclassified opportunities, no multi-touch attribution, and marketing under scrutiny after CMO was let go

after_state.with_revsure: In early implementation - Salesforce integration pending, channel classification in progress, custom attribution models being designed with W-shaped as initial preference, targeting CMO/CFO presentation with visual dashboards focused on channel-level pipeline and bookings
wins_realized: Built demo environment for executive presentation
Established CS relationship with weekly live support model
Historical data import confirmed for planning
Opportunity prioritization view for closed-won deals
open_issues: Salesforce integration blocked by internal IT
Custom attribution model requirements still being defined
LinkedIn impression-level attribution not yet available
Funnel stage definitions being revised by new CMO
Target account field/filter logic broken on leads
CMO may reject MQL-focused reporting entirely
Email marketing channel contaminated by CX/sales emails
Business unit segmentation breaking custom dimensions
Channel classification locked on backend not visible to client
Funnel stage definitions still being finalized
